Attys. RUTH M. BOND, ESQ. and IAN T. LONG, ESQ. of Renne Public Law Group (Tel: 415-848-7200) appearing for defendants the City and County of San Francisco, Christine Hanson, and Denise Fisher.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, CHRISTINE HANSON, AND DENISE FISHER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Motion For Summary Adjudication.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear. Defense counsel have now presented the Court with a motion to disqualify pursuant to C.C.P. Sec. 170.6. The Court indicates its reasons as to why it considers defendants' challenge to be untimely, but will afford the parties the opportunity to brief the issue. The Court indicates that briefing by both sides on the issue is due by September 6, 2019. The Court indicates it will not set a page limit, but requests that the parties be reasonable. The Court sets a hearing on defendants' motion to disqualify pursuant to C.C.P. Sec. 170.6 for September 13, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. The Court continues today's hearing on defendants' motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication to that same date and time to trail the hearing on the motion to disqualify.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides in open court.

The Court modifies and confirms the tentative ruling with modifications.

PLAINTIFFS EILEEN STAFSBERG AND TRACY GRIFFIN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production And Special Interrogatories is DENIED in part and OFF CALENDAR in part in part. The Court appreciates the parties' continued efforts to resolve the discovery disputes outside of court. In response to the supplemental joint submission, the Court holds as follows:

Document Request 1: Motion denied with respect to low-functioning and high-functioning. Subject to defendant's agreement to provide summaries.

Document Request 4: Off calendar, Resolved by the parties.

Document Request 6: The Court agrees that the Request does not seek information regarding low-functioning or high-functioning residents. The Court does not understand how the original Request fails to address Plaintiffs' ability to rebut Defendants' argument that the complaints did not constitute protected activity. Denied, subject to the parties agreement.

Document Request 26: Off calendar, Resolved by the parties.

Special Interrogatory Nos. 1-3: The Court would appreciate clarification regarding how the documents produced to date fail to answer the Requests. Denied subject to agreement by the parties. Denied, subject to the parties agreement.

Special Interrogatory Nos. 7-9, 11: Off calendar, Resolved by the parties.

Special Interrogatory No. 10: Denied. Tentative reversed.

Special Interrogatory Nos. 12, 14, 16: Off calendar, Resolved by the parties.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that complies with CRC 3.1312.
